title: #124 - Kopparväggen är AI-boomens dolda flaskhals - dags för den optiska supercykeln?
summary_md:
# #124 - Kopparväggen är AI-boomens dolda flaskhals - dags för den optiska supercykeln?

**Feed**: montrosepodden  
**Published**: 2026-06-06  
**Market Sentiment**: Positive

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A deep-dive episode with tech profile Anders Storm on the AI infrastructure 'stack', arguing that the real bottleneck for next-generation AI is physical — copper and power cannot keep up, driving a shift toward optics/photonics and a possible 'optical supercycle'. The hosts trace where hyperscaler capex (Goldman Sachs cites $7.6T 2026-2031) flows down the value chain from Nvidia GPUs to ASICs (Broadcom, Marvell), CPUs (AMD), memory and optical players (Lumentum, Coherent, Sivers), framing it as a structural supercycle rather than a pure bubble. Strong conviction on Nvidia and AMD, with optics and materials names flagged as high-interest but already heavily run.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NVDA — Nvidia | **BUY** (Strong)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 80%

Best-in-class GPU leader with 80% growth and 80% gross margins, dominant 90% GPU share, investing internally and in suppliers to defend position.

#### Reasoning

Host explicitly maintains conviction in Nvidia going forward. Continued model training demand keeps general GPUs essential; Nvidia also expands into CPUs (N1X with Microsoft) and funds the optics ecosystem, broadening its moat. AI buildout framed as largest infrastructure cycle in history rather than a bubble.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI model training demand plateaus
- if ASIC players capture meaningfully more share
- if hyperscaler capex contracts

> *"Självklart tror jag på Nvidia framåt, det är inga konstigheter."*

---

### 🟢 AMD — AMD | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Strongest CPU player on TSMC, positioned to gain share as agentic AI shifts the GPU:CPU ratio from ~9:1 toward 1:1.

#### Reasoning

Host says AMD is the CPU name he believes most in because the rise of agentic AI creates far greater CPU demand. AMD has overtaken a struggling Intel and uses TSMC, while agentic workloads materially expand the addressable CPU market. May grow faster and gain more share in this segment.

#### Sell Triggers

- if agentic AI adoption stalls
- if Intel regains process/architecture parity
- if GPU:CPU ratio shift fails to materialize

> *"AMD är väl det som jag tror mest på när det gäller just CPU-delen på grund av Argentic AI."*

---

### 🟠 MRVL — Marvell | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Well-run custom-ASIC and optical-fabric giant; Jensen called it a potential trillion-dollar company; Nvidia invested ~$2B. Already run a lot.

#### Reasoning

Host rates Marvell as a company that does the stack-broadening well, spanning compute, optical fabric and networking, and benefiting from Nvidia's optical-IO investment. Explicitly stresses it is no buy recommendation because the stock has already moved strongly; advises long-term monitoring of the growing ASIC and optical market.

#### Sell Triggers

- if ASIC demand growth disappoints
- if optical-IO/AI Photonics adoption is delayed
- valuation already rich after large run-up

> *"nu har ju de bolagen sprungit jättemycket så det är absolut inget köpråd på något sätt det vi pratar om men att man ska hålla ögonen på"*

---

### 🟠 AVGO — Broadcom | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Dominant custom-ASIC incumbent (~95% share with Marvell), building hyperscaler chips and sitting on the optical layer; already heavily run.

#### Reasoning

Host groups Broadcom with Marvell as the two giants controlling custom silicon and the optical-IO layer, expected to keep growing with the ASIC market. Same caveat applies: stocks have run strongly so it is explicitly not a buy call, but a name to monitor long-term as hyperscaler ASIC demand expands.

#### Sell Triggers

- if hyperscaler in-house chip efforts displace demand
- if ASIC market growth slows
- rich valuation after large run-up

> *"Mavel och Broadcom är ju giganter i det här området"*

---

### 🟠 LITE — Lumentum | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Optics player at center of the AI Photonics revaluation, market cap from $2B to $70B; structural scale-up/scale-out story but already up ~1500%.

#### Reasoning

Host frames Lumentum as the template for the optical supercycle — a long-profitable pluggable-optics firm re-rated as AI Photonics scale-up/scale-out demand emerges, now a Nvidia-backed supplier. Used as benchmark for whether other optics names can follow, but the move has already been very large.

#### Sell Triggers

- if CPO/AI Photonics ramp is delayed
- if copper interconnects extend longer than expected
- valuation stretched after 1500% run

> *"Lumentum har ju gått från 2 miljarder dollar i market cap till 70 miljarder dollar i market cap"*

---

### 🟢 TSM — TSMC | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Everything traces back to TSMC; manufactures for Nvidia (fabless), AMD, Broadcom, Alchip; the foundational foundry of the AI buildout.

#### Reasoning

Host notes that going all the way down the stack, since everything ultimately depends on TSMC, it is a good bet to look at as a company. It manufactures essentially all the leading AI silicon, giving broad, lower-risk exposure to the entire AI hardware supercycle.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI chip demand contracts
- geopolitical/Taiwan supply disruption
- if leading-edge capacity oversupplies

> *"så är det väl ett bra bett att titta på TCMC som ett bolag förstås"*

---

### 🟠 SIVE — Sievers Semiconductor | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Swedish optics/photonics player across much of the value chain; up ~3000% after X discovery; speculated as possible next Lumentum. Host is a shareholder and avoids a recommendation.

#### Reasoning

Host, a former CEO and current shareholder, deliberately gives no recommendation but explains the sector revaluation that lifted Sivers from a $120M cap obscure Swedish name into a global phenomenon spanning multiple value-chain layers, prompting the 'next Lumentum?' question. Highly speculative after an extreme run.

#### Sell Triggers

- if hype-driven re-rating reverses
- if Sivers fails to convert positioning into revenue
- if optical supercycle thesis disappoints

> *"kan det här bli det nästa Lumentum?"*

---

### 🟠 AXTI — AXT | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Deep-stack substrate/materials maker for lasers, several layers below foundries; up ~8000% in a year as a bottleneck play.

#### Reasoning

Host cites AXT as an example of investors digging to the very bottom of the stack into substrate materials and finding extreme bottleneck-driven returns. Speculative — host warns of 'false trails' where there is no real bottleneck or strong competition; margins should be checked before committing.

#### Sell Triggers

- if no genuine supply bottleneck exists
- if competition compresses margins
- extreme valuation after 8000% run

> *"bolag som AXT exempelvis som är upp 8000% sedan ett år tillbaka ungefär"*

---
